claim: The study uses a fixed query-based model-repository collection, not a full Hugging Face census.
denominator: 191,375 repository records in the fixed snapshot
avoid_wording: complete Hugging Face census; all open models

claim: Repository records expose strong platform identifiers but incomplete provenance and scholarly-link metadata.
allowed_wording: metadata completeness in the studied repository snapshot
avoid_wording: platform-wide metadata quality in all Hugging Face records

claim: Family/provenance signals are often usable but rarely sufficient for exact upstream claims.
allowed_wording: strict vs usable public upstream-evidence support

claim: Release evidence is layered: stated signals are common, concrete asset-package evidence is much rarer.
Direct paper-to-Hugging Face repository alignment exists but covers only a subset of papers.
denominator: 225 all exact paper-HF edges / 155 all unique papers; 183 main-analysis edges / 124 main-analysis paper records / 2,214 main-analysis paper records
allowed_wording: paper-repository alignment signals, separated by explicit paper links, model-card back-links, and resolved exact edges
avoid_wording: complete paper-to-repo graph; all 155 linked papers are in the 2,214 main-analysis denominator

claim: Curatability is uneven: many records expose useful signals, but fewer jointly support provenance, concrete release assets, and direct paper-repository linkage.
allowed_wording: 6.1% high curatability under the stated joint-evidence rubric; 18.1% operational curation readiness under the operational rubric
avoid_wording: 18.1% jointly satisfies provenance, concrete release, and direct link criteria; objective reproducibility or preservation success
